#Step 3


![Program Design](img/programdesign.png)

## Copy-paste info in matrices of CO into ABCDs

(cell A1 -> column G)
(row headers (col A) -> column F)
(column headers (row 1) -> column E)
(cell content -> column D)

## Select methods and applications

Add these in columns A, B, C of ABCD matrix

## Themes, components, scope and sequence






-----------------------------------




<!------------------------------------------------->
<!------------------------------------------------->
<!------------------- Old stuff ------------------->
<!------------------------------------------------->
<!------------------------------------------------->



## Task - Process feedback: Revise logic model and matrices 

●	Find a place on campus with your little group
●	Use the feedback you received during the plenary session to enhance both your logic model of change and matrices 
●	Read IM book (pp 345-364) to prepare for the plenary session


## Task – Identify methods and applications 

●	Identify methods and applications to influence the determinants in the desired direction. 
  o	Look at your change objectives organized by determinants (see yesterday’s and today’s matrices and use chapter 6 of the IM book).  
  o	Try to link objectives to theoretical methods. If you are unable to identify methods but able to identify applications, ask yourself – why would it work? The answer will lead you to a method.  
  

## Task - Select applications that match your target group


●	Consider parameters of the methods (considerations for use) and translate your methods into practical applications that match your target group. Distinguish between theoretical methods, theoretical and empirical parameters, and practical applications. Take your population, context and parameters into account.

| Methods | Parameter | Applications |
| --- | --- | --- | 
| .................................. | .................................. | .................................. | 
| .................................. | .................................. | .................................. |
| .................................. | .................................. | .................................. |



## Task - Select applications for environmental agents

●	Consider parameters of the methods and select practical applications for the environmental agents. Distinguish between theoretical methods, theoretical and empirical parameters, and practical applications. 

| Methods | Parameter | Applications |
| --- | --- | --- | 
| .................................. | .................................. | .................................. | 
| .................................. | .................................. | .................................. |
| .................................. | .................................. | .................................. |

## Task - Themes, components, scope and sequence

●	Generate ideas about themes, components, scope and sequence of the program
(see page 355 in the book)
